title: Qualitative Capstone — qualitative analysis of analyst response to AI-introduced corruption

fields.vision: The submitted qualitative capstone on whether structured analyst review can catch AI-introduced evidentiary corruption.
fields.mechanism: It uses fifty matched-pair scenarios, deliberately corrupted evidence packets, Structured Analytic Technique execution, sealed coding lanes, human decision review, triad reconciliation, APEX review rounds, P0/P1/P2 severity closure, G1-G5 checks, per-scenario re-coding notes, and all-fifty claim-level human validation to keep the claim bounded.
fields.built_components: The defense was passed on April 27. The independent review rounds found no blocking or major issues, the packet checks passed, and the qualitative review closed without unresolved objections. The validated empirical claim — phrased in the project's own honest-boundary language in the manuscript — is that the study completed all-fifty claim-level human validation across the hundred-and-fifty decision cells, producing a hundred-and-forty-eight Accept and two Revise decisions, and that the CIA-Stack discipline materially reduces high-uptake of poisoned analysis but does not reach active-resistance levels at the rates the original hypothesis predicted. The open questions are pre-published in the manuscript and confirmed in the independent review: the fifty-two-field per-scenario re-coding lane is deferred and named as deferred; the original-method full execution is not certified; the runtime MAS factorial was not executed; the five independent manual Blue Cell passes were not run; and conclusive CIA-Stack superiority is not claimed unconditionally — it is held conditional on the deception-salience language asymmetry your committee surfaced as Condition 3.
fields.source_utilization: Source use is organized by role: method authorities, domain evidence, technical background, implementation evidence, and claim limits. The page should show why each source matters; direct quotations and stronger factual claims still require exact passage anchors before publication.
fields.plan: The forward path is short. Between now and the paper deadline, the only methodological work is a final reconciliation of a small number of triad-workbook cells; beyond publication, the natural next study lives in Row 14, which extends the design space by varying the type of corruption rather than its presence. Step 01 Triad-workbook reconciliation P0 / P1 recheck on SCN-014 and SCN-041; limited by the independent review, so reconciliation work rather than new coding work. Step 02 Manuscript clearance Manuscript writeup clearing the publication gate at the deadline. Step 03 Future-research extension Row 14 extends the design space by varying the type of corruption rather than its presence — the natural next study after publication.
fields.status: Status: The defense was passed on April 27. The independent review rounds found no blocking or major issues, the packet checks passed, and the qualitative review closed without unresolved objections. The validated empirical claim — phrased in the project's own honest-boundary language in the manuscript — is that the study completed all-fifty claim-level human validation across the hundred-and-fifty decision cells, producing a hundred-and-forty-eight Accept and two Revise decisions, and that the CIA-Stack discipline.
fields.remaining_gaps: Remaining gaps: the forward path is short. Between now and the paper deadline, the only methodological work is a final reconciliation of a small number of triad-workbook cells; beyond publication, the natural next study lives in Row 14, which extends the design space by varying the type of corruption rather than its presence.
fields.detailed_source_vision: An empirical demonstration of when human analysts actually catch AI-supported analytic error during Structured Analytic Technique execution against deliberately corrupted evidence. Fifty matched-pair scenarios — clean evidence packet versus contaminated evidence packet — were coded across two prompt-discipline conditions under an eight-surface validation pyramid. The validated empirical finding is that the CIA-Stack discipline materially reduced high-uptake of poisoned analytical output but achieved active resistance only sixteen percent of the time. The methodological claim, surfaced explicitly in the manuscript and held narrower than the original hypothesis, is that SATs function more reliably as audit trails than as cognitive firewalls — and that the narrowed claim is what the validation pyramid actually supports.
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: The empirical claim is not a single coding pass; it is the result of several checks pointing in the same direction. Two sealed AI coding lanes form the base, a human decision review forms the next tier, a triad alignment workbook reconciles the three coding states, and three independent review rounds form the apex.

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.

public_pitch.what_it_is: The submitted qualitative capstone on whether structured analyst review can catch AI-introduced evidentiary corruption.
public_pitch.distinctive_mechanism: It uses fifty matched-pair scenarios, deliberately corrupted evidence packets, Structured Analytic Technique execution, sealed coding lanes, human decision review, triad reconciliation, APEX review rounds, P0/P1/P2 severity closure, G1-G5 checks, per-scenario re-coding notes, and all-fifty claim-level human validation to keep the claim bounded.
public_pitch.why_it_matters: Its value is not that it proves AI safety generally; it shows a disciplined way to study when human analysts function as a meaningful check on AI-assisted analytic error, while stating that the fifty-two-field re-coding lane, runtime MAS factorial, and full Blue Cell execution remain deferred rather than falsely completed.
public_pitch.pitch_integrity_note: Mechanism-first public pitch inserted after the Nexus Lab mechanism-erasure audit on 2026-05-13; source basis: rendered correction table.

pitch_integrity_audit.standard: object, distinctive mechanism, value, built state, and non-overclaim discipline must be visible.

researcher_process_map.summary: The submitted qualitative capstone on whether structured analyst review can catch AI-introduced evidentiary corruption.
researcher_process_map.final_use_case: Its value is not that it proves AI safety generally; it shows a disciplined way to study when human analysts function as a meaningful check on AI-assisted analytic error, while stating that the fifty-two-field re-coding lane, runtime MAS factorial, and full Blue Cell execution remain deferred rather than falsely completed.
